learning_outcomes:
1. Major Programme:Upon the completion of the Pharmacy programme, the graduate is: (1) Professional knowledge and application (K/S) - prepared with scientific and professional knowledge and skills for
contemporary pharmaceutical practice; - equipped with qualitative and quantitative/statistical analysis
skills, and be able to collect, interpret and manage scientific
and clinical data;- able to adopt a systematic approach in analyzing and solving
problems in the manufacture and quality assurance of pharmaceutical products; (2) Professional attitude and conduct (S/V) - able to accept responsibility for his/her own actions and
decisions; - able to apply relevant ethical and legal principles in the
practice of pharmacy; - able to uphold the professional code of conduct; (3) Professional communication (S) - able to communicate effectively with patients, peers, other health
professionals and the public; - confident and competent in counselling patients and clients on
prescription, non-prescription drugs and consumer health products; (4) Information evaluation and decision-making (K/S) - able to retrieve and critically evaluate medical/pharmaceutical
literature; - able to identify and appropriately utilize drug information
resources to assist with patient-specific drug therapy monitoring; - confident in making decisions on patients’ drug therapy and
recommending appropriate treatment; and (5) Continuing education and lifelong learning (K/S/V) - able to build upon his/her knowledge and skills and engage in
lifelong continuing education and professional development. K = Knowledge outcomes S = Skills outcomes V = Values and
attitudes outcomes
study_scheme:
Pharmacy Pharmacy Applicable to students admitted in 2024-25
Major Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 75 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  10
MEDF1011[a], 1012[a], 1021, 1031
and 1041
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  57
PHAR1000#, 1004, 1110, 1423, 1433,
1434, 1440, 2110, 2131, 2211, 2212, 2213, 2220, 2313, 2320, 2330, 2410,
2520, 3330, 3413, 3414, 3415, 3420, 3510, 3812
3.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  Students are required to complete 6
units of capstone courses from one of the Areas of Concentration.  |  6
Area of Concentration:
Pharmacy Clerkship
(i)  |  PHAR4201, 4301
(ii)  |  2 units of courses chosen from the
following[b]:
PHAR4204, 4205, 4302, 4303, 4304,
4305, 4306, 4307, 4308, 4309, 4310, 4350, 4401, 4402, 4403, 4501, 4601,
4901
Area of Concentration:
Research
(i)  |  PHAR4911, 4912
(b)  |  At least 2 units of courses chosen
from the following[c]:  |  2
PHAR2710, 2711, 2712
Total:  |  75
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  PHAR courses
at 2000 and above level as well as those labeled as # will be included in
the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students are
encouraged to take 1–5 units of free elective courses from PHAR2018,
3603, 3604 and 3605 in the summer session to gain experience in pharmacy
practice. However, these free elective courses shall not be counted
towards the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
3.  |  Students must
obtain Grade "C+" or above in the following courses, including PHAR3603, 3604, 3605, 3812, 4201, 4204, 4205,
4301, 4302, 4303, 4304, 4305, 4306, 4307, 4308, 4309, 4310, 4350, 4401,
4402, 4403, 4501, 4601, 4901 and 4912, in order to fulfill Major
Programme Requirement. Those who obtain Grade "C" or below may
repeat the same course or take a substitute course to fulfill the Major
Programme Requirement in the subsequent year.
4.  |  Students are
required to declare the Area of Concentration in June of the year immediately
before their final year of attendance.
[a]  |  Students
obtained a total score of at least 38 in 6 HKDSE subjects, including at
least 5* in English, Biology and Chemistry, are eligible to apply for
course and unit exemptions for MEDF1011 and MEDF1012, to be granted by
the Board of the Faculty of Medicine on a case-by-case basis on behalf of
the Senate. Students who have been granted
such exemptions are still required to fulfill the overall unit
requirement of the Major Programme.
[b]  |  Only a number of Pharmacy
Clerkship courses will be offered in each academic year in accordance
with resources available in that particular academic year.
[c]  |  Students are
encouraged to take two major elective courses. If students take more than
1 Major Elective Course, the surplus course(s) shall also be counted
towards the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
